title: storage: force_mask not working when UID 0 mapped to subuid
body:
I am trying to use the `force_mask` option with restrictive UID/GID mappings, and I have found that it doesn't work unless UID 0 (in the container) is mapped to the host UID of the user that is running podman.

Here is my `storage.conf`.

```
$ cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf
[storage]
driver = "overlay"

[storage.options.overlay]
force_mask = "shared"
mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
```

If I explicitly map container UID 0 to my host UID (1000), everything works.

```
$ podman run --rm -it \
        \
        --uidmap 0:@1000 \
        --uidmap 42:@500101 \
        --uidmap 499:@500102 \
        \
        --gidmap 0:@500100 \
        --gidmap 4:@500101 \
        --gidmap 5:@500102 \
        --gidmap 8:@500103 \
        --gidmap 42:@500104 \
        --gidmap 43:@500105 \
        --gidmap 50:@500106 \
        --gidmap 499:@500107 \
        \
        docker.io/library/debian:bookworm-slim \
        /bin/sh
# whoami
root
```

However, if I map container UID 0 to a subuid, it doesn't work.

```
$ podman run --rm -it \
        \
        --uidmap 0:@500100 \
        --uidmap 42:@500101 \
        --uidmap 499:@500102 \
        \
        --gidmap 0:@500100 \
        --gidmap 4:@500101 \
        --gidmap 5:@500102 \
        --gidmap 8:@500103 \
        --gidmap 42:@500104 \
        --gidmap 43:@500105 \
        --gidmap 50:@500106 \
        --gidmap 499:@500107 \
        \
        docker.io/library/debian:bookworm-slim \
        /bin/sh
Error: crun: open `/var/tmp/intermediate-mountpoint-1000.0/etc/hosts`: No such file or directory: OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
```

(Mapping a different container UID, e.g. 499, to host UID 1000 produces the same error.)

Here is the full debug output of the failing case.


I am running `podman-5.8.1-1.fc43.x86_64` on Fedora 43.

comments.body:
can you please share the output of `podman info`?

What are the permissions on `/var` and `/var/tmp` (`ls -ldn /var /var/tmp`)?

Have you already tried disabling selinux `--security-opt label=disable`?

On a whim, I used Claude Code to read the discussion in the PR and see if it could figure out why it works on Rawhide and not on Fedora 43.  After much churning, it identified the version of `fuse-overlayfs` as the likely culprit.  I rebuilt the Rawhide package for Fedora 43, and the container now works with with UID 0 mapped to a subuid.

So it looks like it was a bug/limitation in `fuse-overlayfs` all along.  (Uugh!)
